entry.title: Re: What you need to get and stay certified
entry.content:
Hi @nickv23 
Please check our Staying certified website where you find the FAQ

Do I need to have a subscription to SAP Learning Hub to stay certified?
Yes. To stay certified, you must have a subscription to the enhanced and redesigned SAP Learning Hub. With one single subscription, you can get access to all resources you need to prepare for certifications, take the certification exams with four included certification attempts and stay certified with access to stay certified assessments and preparation materials. You can purchase a subscription on SAP Learning Hub or by contacting your SAP representative. SAP PartnerEdge members can learn more about and subscribe to SAP Learning Hub, partner edition.

Alternatively, if you don’t have an SAP Learning Hub subscription, you still have the option to purchase and retake an SAP Certification exam every year.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: A Complete Guide to Comparing Versions in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Excellent blog, very helpful to see the different options to compare versions in SAC Planning. The restricted measures with constant selection was a good trick.

This functionality has been is a huge value add for my team during our annual planning processes. 

Thank you for taking the time to share.

entry.title: Re: Entities Created in SAP CAP UI but Fields Have Null V...
entry.content:
The issue was with the "annotation.cds" file, specifically in the value of idocTypeConfiguration.segList.qualifier. The correct value should simply be qualifier.

To resolve this, I had to remove idocTypeConfiguration.segList from all relevant attributes.

entry.title: Re: SAC Add-in for Excel
entry.content: SAC plugin is a lot worst than Analysis for Office, and slow to refresh data... I would prefer 100 times Analysis fo Office. If SAP will not allow that Analysis for Office connect to SAC models to consume data then it's very annoying.. we only use it to check data in models...

entry.title: Re: What you need to get and stay certified
entry.content:
Hi @Marm 
Please check our How to get certified? 
Threr you find th FAQ
14. Failed your exam?
You can take an SAP Certification exam up to three (3) times. If you do not pass an exam after three (3) attempts, you will need to re-register for the next release of the exam. If you do not pass the next release after three (3) attempts, you will not be able to retake the exam until the next edition is released, normally within 1 year.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Error when uploading Data in the Manage Lookup Table...
entry.content:
This is the whole sheet:

I blurred the information in the first to columns.

Its all the same customer id and just different rateElements. I thought that maybe some of the entrys are mandatory but i dont know which.

entry.title: Any possible side effects to using TOUCHSRC?
entry.content:
Hi all,

Recently we had some dumps with LOAD_PROGRAM_TABLE_MISMATCH related to a column added to a standard table. There are some SAP Notes related to this and one of the recommendations is running the program TOUCHSRC for fixing the program that gave out the error.

My question: Is there any possible problem or side effect using this program (TOUCHSRC)? My main concern is that this would be executed in a production system.

TIA,

Luis

entry.title: Re: Entities Created in SAP CAP UI but Fields Have Null Values and Data Not Inserted in the DB
entry.content: No i don't have handler logic associated with the create event

entry.title: Re: What you need to get and stay certified
entry.content:
Hi @Margit_Wagner 

I'm interested in a certification that is only valid until March 31, 2025. I just want to do the assessment. Does assessment count as a normal exam attempt or can the assessment be repeated several times?

BR

Marm

entry.title: Re: Replace a Model in the Optimized (Unified) Experience
entry.content:
@ChintS I've requested the SAC development team to provide an option to replace Models in SAC Stories for different data Source types such as SAP HANA Live model to SAP Datasphere analytical model, so that we do not have to rebuild the reports that are based on a HANA view. 

The last update I got was a month ago and it says "This is HIGH Priority topic for Development team and they are working on this topic. We can expect some information in 2025 roadmap".

entry.title: How can i expose my BTP CAPM application built with nodejs to external system
entry.content:
Hi

I want to create a CAPM application which will create database entries into Postgresql (BTP backing service) and read data from postgresql DB.  I want this application to be accessible from ABAP system (through request parameter i need to perform DB operations).

Thanks 

Shankar

entry.title: SAP Signavio Process Intelligence
entry.content: Why is it that after successfully transferring data from ServiceNow to SAP Process Intelligence and successfully creating the table to use for analysis, the cycle time doesn't show up in my investigation discovery?

entry.title: Re: Retrieve metadata failed because the OData server ret...
entry.content: I suspect the issue is in the SAP Cloud Connector trust to the on-premise system.  This is done through the use of short lived certificates and requires setup both in SCC and the on-premise system through CERTRULE and possibly STRUST if using self-signed certificates from the SCC.  I would start by verifying your SCC configuration to the host and associated settings.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA for Product Compliance: "Reverse" Outdated supplier status
entry.content:
Hello @Michael_Mulder 

Regarding this subject, is there any correction yet, delivered through an SAP note?
If not, do you think it's feasible, with an experienced ABAP or UI5 consultant, to somehow "block" or even "deleting" this option from the page? 
Thank you in advance

entry.title: Re: Use Chart Trellis in SAP Analytics Cloud Story
entry.content: video still not working @Donatas  can you reload please?

entry.title: Does RSTRAN table exist in BW Bridge?
entry.content:
Hello to everyone.

In one of my project, we are looking for the counts of BW objects(Adso, DS, TRFN, etc.) available in BW Bridge.

In BW/BW/4HANA such information were available in standard tables like RSDS, RSTRAN, etc. 

Does these tables are still relevant in case of BW bridge? if yes, where and how can I find this at database(Schema) level?

entry.title: Global assignment - business rule
entry.content:
Hello dear experts,

We have initiated shot-term assignments for some colleagues who will be staying in Germany for a period of time and have noticed that on the Home profile, the employee group should have automatically changed from Employee to Expatriate and did not. 

We created a business rule but seems to not work as expected. 

Any guidance would be appreciated.

Thank you,

entry.content: Looking forward to the ALM EMEA summit 2025 🙂

entry.title: Re: Wildcard Character for WEBi Search
entry.content:
...MATCHES PATTERN... and 'AB%'  should work.

What is your database from which you are pulling data?

Matches Pattern operator | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Replicate SAP FSM WorkTime (Reservations/Absences) to SAP ECC
entry.content:
Hello experts,

is there a way to replicate work time efforts (absences / reservations) created by a technician in FSM to SAP ECC?

Only option i found is the ProAxia Connector, is there no other option?

Thanks in advance and best regards,

Max

entry.title: Scope Item 6W1 - Freight Unit Building Rule available
entry.content:
Hi,

I am new to SAP TM Public Cloud. I have a question related to the Freight Unit Building Rule App. Is the Freight Unit Building Rule App also available for scope item 6W1 External Transportation Planning ? I checked the App catalogue but I couldn't find any information.

entry.title: Re: Component Allocation for multiple alternative bom in...
entry.content:
Hi, 

similar issue here. 

What we are missing is the window that is visible on your Screenshot, where it is possible to select from maintained BOM alternative. 

We have a Retail System if that should make a difference - 2 BOM Alternatives maintained, but the system always picks BOM alternative 1 with no possibility to intervene/change to BOM version 2. 

We cannot deactivate BOM alternative 1, because they could get used at the same time. 
Is it because the affected material is a phantom assembly?

entry.title: SAPLSLVC_FULLSCREEN Not showing buttions ALV
entry.content:
Hi , 

After sap upgrade from s4/ hana to cloud version , when executing specific ALV Report , some buttons in the toolbar don't appear even though all buttons are active in the painter , what could be the problem , my GUI Status is ZSTANDRAD copy of standard of SAPLSLVC_FULLSCREEN GUI Status

entry.title: Re: What is meant by call off Purchase orders?
entry.content:
Hello @AAkash1 

Call-off purchase orders are know in SAP as contract release orders. These are purchase orders created against a contract - SAP Help Creating a Contract Release Order

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: SAP AIF use Idoc field value as input for a function
entry.content: Hi Britta,

entry.title: From Old to New: Watch Transformation in Action
entry.content:
Transformation is Key: Say goodbye to the old and hello to the new ⚡️

In the Utilities industry, challenges are constant and efficiency is everything. Check out what transformation can do for your business:

1. Build stronger connections 

Discover how SAP solutions for Customer Experience, including SAP Service Cloud for Utilities, transform the way you engage with customers at every stage of their journey. From providing personalized service to handling complex service requests and orders with ease, this solution makes it simpler to overview your data and keep your customers satisfied.

💡Want to learn more? Check out these insightful blogs and articles to see what is now possible: 

  • Working with SAP Service Cloud Version 2 Add-on for Utilities: Master Data Replication Part I 
  • Working with SAP Service Cloud Version 2 Add-on for Utilities

2. Optimize operations 

Experience how easily it is now to enhance workforce management and improve customer satisfaction in your utility company by digitizing service and asset management processes with SAP solutions for Service and Asset Management, with a ready-to-use platform to automate workforce scheduling and efficiency with AI support. 

💡Looking for more details? Check out in-depth resources: 

  • SAP Field Service Management: GenAI, Enhanced User Experience, and Improved Integration 
  • Enhance Your Maintenance and Field Technicians' Efficiency and Productivity with SAP Dynamic Forms

3. Make real-time decisions with real-time data

In the data-driven future of utilities, SAP S/4HANA Utilities and FIORI Apps offer a clear, real-time view of your processes. From metering and billing to invoicing, easily track progress, spot issues, and make quick decisions with user-friendly visuals - all of this in just three easy-to-follow levels, replacing the need for multiple steps. 

💡Want to dive deeper? Explore the latest updates and innovations:

  • SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS2: What's in it for the Utilities Industry?
  • SAP S/4HANA Utilities Demo Series 

Discover What's Possible...

Step into the future with solutions that make a real difference and embrace the benefits:

  • Improved efficiency and productivity 
  • Increased agility and flexibility
  • Enhanced customer experience 
  • Competitive advantage
  • Latest user experience 

Transformation is more than just change - it's about making your life and work easier. Watch this video series to see how our latest, user-friendly solutions can simplify daily tasks for you, your teams, and your customers.

Stay tuned for more to come! ⚡

 

Best regards,

Your ️SAP Energy & Utilities Brand Awareness team

entry.title: Re: SAP AIF use Idoc field value as input for a function
entry.content: Hi Britta, we are on S/4HANA 2020 Public Cloud.

entry.title: Re: Forecast schedule lines vs. Planned schedule lines in...
entry.content:
Thank yo for your request, @alaintgkm ,

please have a look at the flow diagram of business scope item 3NR which shows how forecasts vs planning data are being processed in SAP best practices.

Also, please have a good look at the Accelerators of this scope item, mainly the set-up instructions and test script:

 

I got to the scope item in the SAP for Me Process Navigator via the SAP Business Accelerator Hub for the API you had mentioned, API Reference | Delivery Schedule of Sales Scheduling Agreement – Receive, Update (B2B) | SAP Business Accelerator Hub which has many more details about the API proper and its business context.

I noticed your request had been lingering in the Community for a bit. In future, please use tag "SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition" for respective requests as this tag is monitored regularly by our experts. Thank you.

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace -Send shipment status to customer/buyer using easy r
entry.content:
Hello Elaine,

See my answers,

1. I am not sure that is possible in an e-mail. What is the use case of button Vs hyperlink?

2. Use place holder   ${BP_LOGO}. This will replace with logo image from 'Manage Business Profile' app.

3. You can have images(static) added in Manage Notification Template app email template

4. Use Editor capability within Manage Notification Template app

entry.title: Re: Replicate SAP FSM WorkTime (Reservations/Absences) to...
entry.content: As of today, this is the only way to handle WTP, reservations, and absences. However, there is some work happening on the integration front; please check the roadmap for more details. SAP Road Map Explorer

entry.title: 360 reviews grouped
entry.content: In AVC we need to show the results of the 360 reviews grouped by role in the summary report that comes with the form. Have any of you managed to configure the aggregated result?

entry.title: Re: FSM - Planning & Dispatching - Activities List
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

Yes, the behavior you described aligns with the design of the Planning & Dispatching function. The Activities tab was specifically designed to display only those activities that pertain to the chosen planning scenario in the Dispatching board. Therefore, currently there is no available setting to view all activities regardless of the planning scenario selected.

However, we are open to customer feedback and suggestions for improvement. Therefore, if you would like the ability to view all activities in the Activities tab, we would encourage you to open an influence at https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/1608. Here, you can submit with a use case for this possible amendment. Your suggestion will then be reviewed and potentially considered for inclusion in future software updates.

 

Thanks & Best Regards,
Ziad Mamlouk

entry.title: Re: Step by Step process to download EDIFACT XSDs from Integration Advisor for B2B Integration in CI

Thanks Adharsh,

Any guidance would be of great help!

Cheers!

entry.title: Impact of ignoreErrors on Active Index in SolrIndexConfig
entry.content:
In the SolrIndexConfig, there is a property called ignoreErrors. According to the documentation, this property allows the index to continue running even if failures occur.

My question is: Will setting ignoreErrors=true and running an index-Job also change the active index, even if the index is not complete?
Or will it simply run through and log all the errors without making any changes to the active index?

entry.title: Re: Error VPK1310 in DP93 - Intercompany Resource Related...
entry.content: If you are in S4, carry out code corrections as recommended in SAP note 3277686

entry.title: Re: SAP Universal ID S users - issue
entry.content: @alessia_pozzi Thank you for the update! I am glad to hear it got sorted 🙂

entry.title: Re: Reference field for doc. Type KX doesn't appear in FBL1N
entry.content:
Resolution

Please follow the below steps.

1. Regenerate the structure POSTAB.

  •      For FBL*N, Call transaction SE38 and execute the program RFPOSXEXTEND.
  •      For FAGLL03, Call transaction SE37, enter the function module ITEM_STRUC_EXTENSION and execute it with the following parameters:

                BASIC_STRUCNAME       -->    FAGLPOSY
                EXT_STRUCNAME           -->    FAGLPOSYEXT
                EXT_FIELDS_TABNAME   -->    T021S
                I_LSTCL                              -->   D
                X_TRANSPORT                -->    ' '

2. Clear the buffer to get the values populated.

  • Call transaction SE38 and execute the program BALVBUFDEL.

Now re-run the transactions FBL*N/FAGLL03, check for those special fields available for selection in change layout and see the values displayed for the same.

entry.title: Re: How can I add code formatting in a comment in response to a question on the SAP Community page?
entry.content: To confirm quickly. Enabling the full editor for comments is on our enhancement request list.

entry.title: Re: Copy control is missing AF. Message -VL473
entry.content: If you were trying to create delivery with reference to a quotation, then, it is not possible.  You need to create a sales order with reference to the quotation and then, with reference to that sales order, system will allow to create delivery.

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?
entry.content:
Treat to meet you. I live in the US outside of Philadelphia. My role is a community advocate. Your topics are fascinating. Be sure to join Americas | Location-Based Groups | SAP Community and the many interest groups.
Also don't miss the upcoming virtual event: SAP Business Unleashed in 2025 | Virtual Event

entry.title: Re: Setting up favorites in SAP Build WorkZone - Standard...
entry.content: That's correct, but only for classic display modes. The new site experience, using spaces, pages and UI integration cards, unfortunately is not yet able to use any personalization features.

entry.title: Re: A warm welcome to our new SAP Champions in January 2025
entry.content: Congratulations, and welcome to all new SAP Champions! 🎉

entry.title: Understanding the Recruiting to Onboarding Mapping and troubleshooting
entry.content:
This article was built together with @CarolinaCLeite. Here, we summarized the main recommendations and suggestions for Recruit-To-Hire Data Mapping.

For the data flow from Recruiting to Onboarding, we need to take into account some assumptions:

  • Onboarding shares the Succession Data Model with EC
  • Fields marked as required in the Succession Data Model are required in the mapping

In this article, you can learn how to set up the mapping properly. 

To trigger Onboarding from Recruiting, it's only necessary to map a subset of info needed to start the Onboarding processes. However, the mapping is based on the ONB/EC data model, and all fields required in the data model will be required for mapping. 

To avoid adding these in Recruiting, where it would make no sense to have some of that specific data, you can follow several strategies.

 

First Scenario:

Use person types to make the fields required in the UI but not for the mapping

Example: "Preferred Name" should be derived via business rule in ONB/EC, or be required in the UI, but not mapped from Recruiting

Step 1: Make the field ‘Preferred Name’ in personalinfo non mandatory in the BCUI 

Step 2: Create an Employee Person Type and an Onboardee Person Type, and make the field mandatory

Step 3: Preferred Name is not required in Recruit-To-Hire Data Mapping

Not all portlets allow the creation of person types. Below you can find a list of not supported ones. For these cases, review the second and third scenarios and "other".

 

HRIS ElementAction/Notes
compInfo There is no employee type 
Job Relationship TypeThere is no employee type
payComponent (Recurring & Non-Recurring)There is no employee type 
User Accounts There is no employee type

 

Second Scenario:

Make the field not required in the BCUI. Create a rule to change the properties of the field to make it required in the UI.

Step 1: Make these fields not required in the portlet in the BCUI (as in the first scenario)

Step 2: Create a rule to set the "required" property to "true"

For more info on how to set up the rule in ONB/EC data model, please refer to the blog https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blogs-by-members/using-business-rules-to-set-field-visibility-required-attributes/ba-p/13501339 

 

Third Scenario:

Create dummy fields in Recruiting and use these in the mapping.

Step 1: Create both a text field and a date field in Recruiting (Requisition or Application templates), invisible to all operators. Name them "Dummy text field for ONB mapping" and "Dummy date field for ONB mapping" (or something similar). Make sure you configure the fields in all requisition or application templates.

Step 2: Run an OData Metadata Refresh to make these fields show in Recruit-To-Hire Data Mapping

Step 3: Map the text field to string or object fields in Onboarding, and the date field to date fields

 

Other topics and recommendations

Specific fields added for Onboarding when you have multiple Recruiting templates

Let's focus on an example. You would like to add two fields, 'Email type' and 'Primary Flag', to the mapping. These fields are not visible to the Recruiter and candidate, and you pre-filled them with a business rule. However, the mapping is not working. One possible reason is that you have added the fields to only one Job Requisition template but there are two templates active and used in Recruiting. In this case, you need to add your fields to all of the templates, because the mapping won't work with one template only .

Pay Component mapping: 

The "amount" should be defined as type "currency" in the offer template. It's only necessary to map the pay component value - not the currency, frequency, or pay component code.

Follow Approach 2 from the KBA 3204900 - [Onboarding] Multiple pay components. The scenario in Approach 2 is the recommended one from release B2111.

Currency - the value is taken from the FO if you don't map it in Recruit-to-Hire Data Mapping. If the value is not available in the FO, then the value for the field is taken from the legal entity. If the value is not available in the legal entity, then the value for the currency-code field is taken from the legal entity country.

Frequency - the values for the Pay Component Recurring and Pay Component Non-Recurring fields are populated from the FO. If not maintained, an error is created in Business Process Engine (BPE).

Alternatively, Currency and Frequency can be set up with business rules in EC.

 

Troubleshooting

A feature to troubleshoot errors in the mapping between Recruiting and Onboarding is available in the Check Tool. Go to Check Tool -> Validation and select "Onboarding" under "Application". Select "Data mapping between Recruiting Management and Onboarding is consistent. (ONB2DataValidationCheckForInitiateOnb)" and then "Run Check". Add the application ID and click "Run". If there are errors in the mapping, the system will provide clear details.

For more details and additional options please review the blog https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blogs-by-sap/checks-to-troubleshoot-issues-related-to-initiate-onboarding-from/ba-p/13552680

entry.title: Re: aseuserstore and dbisql
entry.content:
Hi,

This app works without username and password, but correct host:port so the property is picked up from the advanced tab.
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ASE_SDK/c4016b5564ed4c98bbade36a3a35202d/b0822ab9bbf91014ba1bc4fea5c9aafb.html?q=SECURE_STORE_KEY&locale=en-US

Bad host:port will give you:
JZSCF: Conflicting information encountered in secure store.

This behavior is documented and shows how to do this with standalone code:

Example 2

The following example shows the usage of SECURE_STORE_KEY property through a connection URL string. Here, the connection will be established with localhost:1600 if the connection information for ASE1TESTUSER is also specified as localhost:1600, else it results in an error because there is a mismatch between secure store and the connection string.

Code Syntax

DriverManager.getConnection("jdbc:sybase:Tds:localhost:1600?SECURE_STORE_KEY=ASE1TESTUSER);
Example 3
Code Syntax

In the following example, the host and port information is not provided in the connection string. In this scenario, the driver uses the connection information present in the secure store.


DriverManager.getConnection("jdbc:sybase:Tds:?SECURE_STORE_KEY=ASE1TESTUSER);

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ASE_SDK/c4016b5564ed4c98bbade36a3a35202d/17c13174c0de42fb92adb65ecbe4dc91.html?q=SECURE_STORE_KEY&locale=en-US

I've created a feature request for aseuserstore and dbisql/interactive sql.
Looks like we do not parse or use the alternative url for this product.
CR#830317

Regards,
Ryan

entry.title: Drill-thru to S/4 tcode from a SAC table
entry.content:
Hi Team,

 

My customer wants the ability to drill thru from a SAC table to a transaction code (Ex: KSB1) in S/4 using parameters.

 

Is this possible using SAC Planning table? If so, please do share your inputs.

 

Thank you.

 

Regards,

 

Prasad Rangudu

entry.title: How to Use Statistics Functions in SAP PCo
entry.content:
Hello All!

I am currently working on a project using SAP Plant Connectivity (PCo) and would like to understand how to use the Statistics Functions available, such as Integral (time-weighted total), Max, and Min within specific subintervals. (https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_PLANT_CONNECTIVITY/c90214be0d934ebdb6f3bce29c63c0ff/0246c884fc27485e85850b177362d5b3.html?q=Integral+(time-weighted+total)

Here’s my current setup:

  • I have both the PI SDK and PI AF SDK installed properly.
  • I am using PI AF as the data source and, in some cases, PI directly as the source system.

However, while exploring the PCo documentation and interface, I couldn’t find where or how to configure and use these statistical functions. My goal is to process historical data and apply these functions to calculate values based on specific time subintervals.

I’d greatly appreciate any guidance or practical examples that the community can share!

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Custom Value Help Creation For a Perticular Field In...
entry.content: It seems you created this in the Q&A Section, but you should put in the Blogs by Members section.

entry.title: Re: SAP AIF use Idoc field value as input for a function
entry.content: Hi Pascal, as you are on a 2020 release have a look at the key field navigation feature. https://help.sap.com/docs/ABAP_PLATFORM_NEW/4db1676c3f114f119b500bd80ccd944d/80f98b77399840b8badfc99c13c2ae68.html?version=202009.latest&locale=en-US It might solve your problem.

entry.title: Do you know how to implement and how to manage SAP Cell and Gene Therapy Orchestration?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Implementing and Configuring SAP Cell and Gene Therapy Orchestration learning journey.

Overview
This course will guide you through the necessary steps to configure your SAP Cell and Gene Therapy Orchestration tenants. It includes setting up the SAP Business Technology Platform and managing necessary master data.​

Learning objectives
This course will prepare you to: 

  • ​Deploy SAP Cell and Gene Therapy Orchestration and set up SAP Business Technology Platform
  • ​Configure SAP Cell and Gene Therapy Orchestration
  • ​Manage required Master Data​ ​

Prerequisites

  • Exploring the Foundations of SAP Cell and Gene Therapy Orchestration

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 
Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: aseuserstore and dbisql
entry.content: Thanks for creating ChangeRequest 830317!

entry.title: Re: How to check change logs in table CDHDR and or Utilizing CDHDR and CDPOS SAP Tables for Change
entry.content: In most of the cases, we do not know from which user id, the changes has happened and on which date.  Having said that your screen shot shows only these two fields and if the outcome is only few entries, you cannot straightaway, conclude only those changes has happened.  It is always better to maintain OBJECTCLAS in CDHDR so that data would be fetched at the quickest possible time.

entry.title: Re: The SQL Anywhere for PC Platforms page shows up to Wi...
entry.content: Thank You for the reply.

entry.title: Re: Wildcard Character for WEBi Search
entry.content: Hi Michael, thanks for the reply. Yes, I have tried that combination as well with no luck. My data source is SAP HANA.

entry.title: Re: SAP Learning HUB
entry.content:
Hi @ASAMAD 
Please access our Activation code FAQs 
You find a a detailed answer to the question
How do I redeem my activation code for SAP Learning Hub?
Go to training.sap.com/redeem, enter the activation code and choose “Validate.”
To validate your code, you must have an SAP user account. If you have one, then choose “Log on” and enter your SAP Universal ID. If you don’t have an SAP user account, choose "Create SAP User Account."

For more information on activating your code, see the SAP Activation Code Redemption Guide 

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: where is Crystal Reports Java Reporting Component Dev...
entry.content: not an answer

entry.title: Re: SAP Crystal Reports for Eclipse v2 EOMM
entry.content: SP31 got released and is available to download, but I don't find any release notes

entry.title: Re: I am trying to create a virtual element for the CDS view and need to get the output as string or
entry.content:
@ObjectModel.virtualElement: true

@EndUserText.label : 'Serial Number'

@AnalyticsDetails.query.axis: #COLUMNS

@Semantics.text: true

@ObjectModel.virtualElementCalculatedBy: 'ZCL_OTC_0365_GET_SERIAL' 

serialNumber cast( '0' as abap.char(255)) as serialNumber  --- this shows no error 

 

but If I write serialNumber cast( ' ' as abap.char(255)) as serialNumber) , it errors out, note that we need to concatenate all the serial numbers and put it here 

I have attached the image, also note that localized is not working, it might be due to the fact that it is a Consumption view.

entry.title: Re: Creation of WBS using CNMASSCREATE
entry.content:
Hi Arpitchauhan;

In the template available for download in CNMASSCREATE, there are two fields at the end of the WBS Elements tab:
- Superior WBS Element
- Project Definition

It is possible that your download failed.

Best Regards
Marcio Blos

entry.title: App "Manage Prices - Sales" - created by user and changed by user information
entry.content:
Hi all,

is there a possibility to add the "created by" and "changed by" as a filter or in the table of results in the FIORI App "Manage Prices - Sales"? 

For our customer, this is a crucial information for condition records. 

In the GUI-App "Set material prices - Sales" there is a change log for that information so it should be available somehow.

Thanks and best regards

Katja

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?

Thank for the invitation to join in such interesting groups and I am subscribing now for the virtual event that will take place in February.

Have a great week!

entry.title: Re: Identity and Access Management with Microsoft Entra, Part I: Managing access to SAP BTP
entry.content:
Hello Experts

@MartinRaepple, @0021239203 @H_Ettelbrueck  - Many Thanks for support.

I have completed the configuration with ODIC. It works. Thank you for the guide.

I have one question: When users log in to SAP BTP, the email address is populated in the "User Name" field. How can I populate a different attribute, such as UPN from Azure, into the "User Name" field in BTP?

And the second question: I can only log in in BTP  with the UPN (firstname.lastname@mail.com). Logging in with the email address doesn't work. What did I do wrong?

 

 

Many Thanks

Best Regards

 

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Why I can't enter SAP roadmap viewer
entry.content:
Your link is a redirection, maybe you have an issue with it.

The current official address is https://me.sap.com/roadmapviewer.

You need to login with an SAP P-User or S-User.

Another possibility is that you get a popup which is blocked, and you need to deactivate the popup blocker for sap.com.

entry.title: CIP Settlement Issue KD 262
entry.content:
User is trying to run settlement and receiving the following error however there is nothing to settle on the order. 

Please assist.

entry.title: Azure Cloud Hotfolder not fetching data with WSL
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm trying to connect to my Sap Commerce Cloud Hotfolder using my local machine (WSL) to download some data for my local environment configuration. I've followed this guide https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_COMMERCE_CLOUD_PUBLIC_CLOUD/403d43bf9c564f5a985913d1fbfbf8d7/4abf9290a64f43b59fbf35a3d8e5ba4d.html?q=cloud.hotfolder.default.mapping.root.dir: 

I created a folder called "myfolder" inside "hybris/master/hotfolder" using Azure Storage Explorer and set these properties:

cluster.node.groups=integration,yHotfolderCandidate
azure.hotfolder.storage.account.connection-string=DefaultEndpointsProtocol=http;AccountName=devstoreaccount1;AccountKey=mykey;BlobEndpoint=http://127.0.0.1:10000/devstoreaccount1;QueueEndpoint=http://127.0.0.1:10001/devstoreaccount1;TableEndpoint=http://127.0.0.1:10002/devstoreaccount1
azure.hotfolder.storage.container.hotfolder=${tenantId}/hotfolder/myfolder


I uploaded the "test.csv" file like stated in the guide but the only log I get on my Hybris instance is this:

 

 

Acquiring lock for key [7c67706a-d0d1-3e8a-b38e-1693a2906c22] in region [yCloudHotfolders] for node [0].
DEBUG [HotfolderLeaderInitiator-junit-1] (junit) [HybrisLockRepository] Acquiring lock for key [7c67706a-d0d1-3e8a-b38e-1693a2906c22] in region [yCloudHotfolders] for node [0].
DEBUG [HotfolderLeaderInitiator-junit-1] (junit) [HybrisLockRepository] Deleting lock records for key [7c67706a-d0d1-3e8a-b38e-1693a2906c22] in region [yCloudHotfolders] older than [2025-01-21T17:20:48.979+0100].
DEBUG [HotfolderLeaderInitiator-junit-1] (junit) [HybrisLockRepository] Deleting lock records for key [7c67706a-d0d1-3e8a-b38e-1693a2906c22] in region [yCloudHotfolders] older than [2025-01-21T17:20:48.979+0100].
DEBUG [HotfolderLeaderInitiator-junit-1] (junit) [HybrisLockRepository] Updating record for lock [lockKey=7c67706a-d0d1-3e8a-b38e-1693a2906c22,region=yCloudHotfolders,clusterId=0,timestamp=Tue Jan 21 17:21:18 CET 2025].
DEBUG [HotfolderLeaderInitiator-junit-1] (junit) [HybrisLockRepository] Updating record for lock [lockKey=7c67706a-d0d1-3e8a-b38e-1693a2906c22,region=yCloudHotfolders,clusterId=0,timestamp=Tue Jan 21 17:21:18 CET 2025].
DEBUG [HotfolderLeaderInitiator-master-1] [LockRegistryLeaderInitiator] Acquiring the lock for LockContext{role=yHotfolderServices, id=master-0, isLeader=true}
DEBUG [HotfolderLeaderInitiator-master-1] [LockRegistryLeaderInitiator] Acquiring the lock for LockContext{role=yHotfolderServices, id=master-0, isLeader=true}
DEBUG [HotfolderLeaderInitiator-master-1] [HybrisLockRepository] Acquiring lock for key [7c67706a-d0d1-3e8a-b38e-1693a2906c22] in region [yCloudHotfolders] for node [0].
DEBUG [HotfolderLeaderInitiator-master-1] [HybrisLockRepository] Acquiring lock for key [7c67706a-d0d1-3e8a-b38e-1693a2906c22] in region [yCloudHotfolders] for node [0].
DEBUG [HotfolderLeaderInitiator-master-1] [HybrisLockRepository] Deleting lock records for key [7c67706a-d0d1-3e8a-b38e-1693a2906c22] in region [yCloudHotfolders] older than [2025-01-21T17:20:50.944+0100].
DEBUG [HotfolderLeaderInitiator-master-1] [HybrisLockRepository] Deleting lock records for key [7c67706a-d0d1-3e8a-b38e-1693a2906c22] in region [yCloudHotfolders] older than [2025-01-21T17:20:50.944+0100].
DEBUG [HotfolderLeaderInitiator-master-1] [HybrisLockRepository] Updating record for lock [lockKey=7c67706a-d0d1-3e8a-b38e-1693a2906c22,region=yCloudHotfolders,clusterId=0,timestamp=Tue Jan 21 17:21:20 CET 2025].
DEBUG [HotfolderLeaderInitiator-master-1] [HybrisLockRepository] Updating record for lock [lockKey=7c67706a-d0d1-3e8a-b38e-1693a2906c22,region=yCloudHotfolders,clusterId=0,timestamp=Tue Jan 21 17:21:20 CET 2025].

 

 

 

How do i test if my local machine is connected to the hotfolder correctly? I tried the same with my customer's Sap Commerce Cloud instance hotfolder but i still didn't get to connect correctly

entry.title: Re: Why I can't enter SAP roadmap viewer

entry.title: Re: O que vocês acham da SAP community e das funcionalidades existentes?
entry.content:
Olá,

Muito interessante o bate papo entre vocês. Me juntei recentemente a SAP Community, embora sempre tenha acompanhado os fóruns principalmente para resolução de problemas do dia - dia. No meu caso, que trabalho com foco no SAP Business One está sendo muito interessante para descobrir mais do universo da SAP e as demais ferramentas que fazem parte da empresa.

entry.title: Re: SAPLSLVC_FULLSCREEN Not showing buttions ALV
entry.content: Well, if you didn't change the ALV report, it's not due to the upgrade "from S/4HANA to Cloud", which is not really an upgrade as it can be only private cloud, not public cloud, so it must be either a backup from on-premises and installation in cloud, possibly with a new SPS version. If you didn't change anything at your custom report, look at the SAP notes or contact the SAP Support.

entry.title: How Offboarding can support Long Leave of Absence
entry.content:
In many meetings with customers and partners, @CarolinaCLeite and I get the request to manage Offboarding for Long Leave of Absence. In this article, you can find what we do to support customers in that scenario.

We often get the request for employees who leave for long leave of absence to return assets and do knowledge transfer with colleagues that are to replace them during the absence. In this case, a sort of short Offboarding process can support from a process perspective.

Step 1: Add the Long Leave of Absence event reason to the post save rule to trigger Offboarding.

Step 2: Create a process variant for this specific process and assign it the rule. In our example, we added only tasks. 

Step 3: Create a specific program for this process and assign it via rule.

Step 4: Update the process flows and perform an OData Metadata Refresh

Step 5: Proxy as an HR admin with permission to manage the event, and add a long leave of absence. Offboarding will be triggered.

entry.title: Re: Authentication Issues occur when sending mail to outl...
entry.content: Hello. I have de same issue.

entry.title: When will Quote 1.0 stop being supported?
entry.content: Is there any set date or time frame for when Quote 1.0 will be fully phased out, and existing clients will need to migrate from Quote 1.0 to Quote 2.0?

entry.title: Re: Unable to generate PRs for operations in a Work Order
entry.content:
hello @sebiemb,

Please check the change log of the maintenance order in the production system. User might have changed the PR triggering point from Never to Immediate / From Release in the operation dropdown. In SPRO it is a default setting. It can be changed in maintenance order screen manually.

If nothing works then check if any enhancement is done related to PR triggering. But chances are rare.

Vaibhav

entry.title: Re: Implementation of Restrictions for Tyre and Battery I...
entry.content:
Hello @mohit9210 ,

You can uncheck the checkbox equipment installation allowed after installing 10 equipment to the FLOC. You will find this checkbox under the structure tab in the FLOC master data. You can identify such FLOCs in the IL05 report then run a LSMW to to remove the flag from this checkbox. But if you want this feature to be activated automatically while loading the data then you need to do enhancement. Try with user exit : ILOM0001. 

Regards,

Vaibhav Mishra

entry.title: SAP Ariba Source to Contract 2502 Release Key Innovations Preview
entry.content:
Hello SAP Ariba Source to Contract Community and welcome to our 2502 key innovations blog.

We're thrilled to unveil the latest features in our Q1 2025 release for SAP Ariba's source-to-contract solutions. These innovations are designed to help you maximize business value, improve supplier relationships, and streamline your procurement processes. Let's dive right in.

SAP Spend Control Tower

Travel & Expense dashboard, presenting information from Concur (ARBTS-56)

With the new Travel & Expense Dashboard, synchronization with SAP Concur is seamless, delivering detailed insights into your travel and expense spend category. This feature covers expense types, payment modes, payment status, and spend categories through a user-friendly interface, pushing the boundaries of analysis efficiency. Understanding your organization's travel and expense spends is now more straightforward, enabling you to make informed decisions quickly.

Support for multiple currencies (ARBTS-629)

As we appreciate that global organizations grapple with managing invoices across a myriad of currencies, we've enhanced SAP Spend Control Tower to support conversion into multiple reporting currencies. Users can now configure their profiles to set the preferred reporting currency for dashboard data – aligning with your financial management strategies and facilitating accurate reporting. This feature provides transparency on global spend, aiding in discovering untapped value potential.

 

SAP Ariba Category Management

AI-assisted Category Strategy Recommendations (CATMAN-467)

SAP Ariba Category Management collects, digitizes and standardizes relevant information from various knowledge bases using AI to provide initial category strategy recommendations.

This new GenAI-powered solution empowers you to save significant time by automating the analysis of category and market dynamics, and leveraging best practices to develop an intelligently structured category strategy that meets stakeholders’ goals and requirements.

 

Supplier Preferencing Matrix (ARBCAT-726)

We're introducing a powerful tool for category managers—the Supplier Preferencing Matrix. This two-dimensional matrix allows you to evaluate how valuable your business is to your suppliers. By plotting the attractiveness of the buyer on one axis and the relative value of the account on the other, you can categorize suppliers and enhance relationships, improve engagement, and manage interactions more effectively.

Operational Reporting APIs for SAP Ariba Category Management (ARBCAT-713)

Great news for those who love data-driven decision-making. Our new Operational Reporting API, built on OpenAPI specifications and leveraging the OData standard, enables you to retrieve operational data from SAP Ariba Category Management. Now, you can access strategy and plan documents, categories with product classifications, and category profiles using external reporting tools. Comprehensive data analysis is just a click away.

The SAP Ariba Category Management APIs are published on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

 

SAP Ariba Guided Sourcing

Support for Large-Capacity Events in Guided Sourcing (SS-58288)

We've enhanced Guided Sourcing to support large-capacity RFP events. You can now manage non-competitive RFPs with up to 10,000 line items, 700 suppliers, and 50 terms per line item using Excel spreadsheets.

Line Item Details in Approval Tasks (SS-62650)

Attention to detail matters. That's why we've added the ability to include line item details in approval tasks sent from SAP Ariba Sourcing to the SAP Task Center. Keep everyone in the loop with clear, concise information that drives better decision-making.

 

SAP Ariba Contract Management

Effortless Line Item Management in Large-Capacity CLID (SC-22523)

Managing large-capacity Contract Line Items Documents (CLID) just got easier. With our new Simplified Excel Import functionality, buyers can import and export line item data, including service line items and pricing conditions, using a preformatted Excel file.

Custom Category Data for Supplier Risk Exposure (SC-22524)

SAP Ariba Contracts now integrates with SAP Ariba Supplier Risk and SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance solutions to provide custom category views of supplier risk exposure data. Combine company data, external data from third-party providers, and more to create up to 25 active custom risk categories per site. This feature ensures that risk management is tailored to your unique business needs.

 

Once, these items are officially released on February 14th, the feature reference IDs (i.e. SC-22524) can help to find more detailed information on http://help.sap.com.

We are continually working to enhance SAP Ariba solutions to ensure you achieve the best results for your business. We hope these new features and enhancements help you streamline your procurement processes, making them more efficient and effective. Stay tuned for more updates and happy sourcing.

We invite you to explore these features in more detail in our SAP Ariba 2502 Release Key Innovations Preview session. Also, stay tuned for our additional upcoming blog posts and follow me to stay informed on upcoming feature releases across SAP Ariba.

Thank you for being a part of the SAP Ariba community and we look forward to continuing our journey of innovation together!

Resources:

  • Feature related materials
    • SAP Ariba Connect - Release Readiness (Customers except CIM)
    • Readiness Community page (For CIM customers)
  • SAP Community for Intelligent Spend Management page
  • SAP Help Portal pages - Procurement and Network
  • What’s New

 

  • Want to see more? Take a look at
    • SAP Products and Services YouTube channel - https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLWV533hWWvDk7zRbhOsWQkF5BGWokhHYH
    • "Spend Management and Business Network" playlist - https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLWV533hWWvDk7zRbhOsWQkF5BGWokhHYH&si=zTlYobcledkWNQ2M

entry.title: Re: Please advise how to write on WBS Budget Availability...

Dear @GiorgioDep,

yes, the same approach is working for WBS elements. I confirm that the using the BUDGET01 version should be used for project Budget Availability Control.

Please also have a look at Setting Up Availability Control for your projects.

Best regards
Susanne

entry.title: Not able to post good receipt and GRPO document, system is generating error
entry.content:
Hello Community ,

When selecting add button in Good Receipt & GRPO document, system is showing this error "Cannot proceed; define inventory account for warehouse, item group, or item" need guidance for this.

entry.title: Re: Custom Value Help Creation For a Perticular Field In...
entry.content: Thanks, sure do

entry.title: Re: Suggestions on Updating Breakdown Notifications and E...
entry.content:
hello @mohit9210,

Access of IE02 to end users is not advisable. here is the alternative -> Create a new layout in IW29 report with only equipment, equipment description, Breakdown checkbox, plant, planning plant and location details. SAVE this layout. Do not show the notification no. and other notification relevant field. This will show the list of equipment which are under breakdown. You can also set up a background job for this layout and send the output file to client email box daily. Client will be able to see the list of equipment under breakdown in his / her email box. For 35 sites you need to save multiple layouts and multiple background jobs.

Logically the maintenance history, breakdown information should remain with the notification and order. Master data should not be updated with it. Master data should be updated when there is a change in the engineering design in it such as change in installation, changes in specifications etc. 

Hope this will help you.

Vaibhav Mishra

entry.title: Re: Query Regarding Row Count of ECC Tables via DVM in SA...
entry.content: Hi SAP Support Team,

entry.title: Re: Prefilling Fields When Creating a New Entity with CAP Node.js
entry.content:
Hello @MioYasutake ,

Nice blog, thanks for sharing the implementation. 

There might be another option, that is placing a Custom Handler for the Before CREATE in the Draft entity. 

Like below 

 

        this.before('CREATE', 'DemoPro.drafts', (req)=> {  
            req.data.Field1 = 'XXXX'
            req.data.Field2 = true
        })

entry.title: Re: Query Regarding Row Count of ECC Tables via DVM in SA...
entry.content: Hi SAP Support Team, I have reviewed the provided link related to SAP Solution Manager’s Data Volume Management (DVM) functionality, but it does not seem to address the of tables row counts or provide any hints on comparing table data across different ECC systems. Could you kindly clarify if it is possible to use the DVM functionality for the row count of tables or data comparison between tables in different ECC systems? If this is not a feature provided by DVM, I would appreciate any guidance on alternative methods to achieve this, if yes, please confirm.

entry.title: SAP Commerce Migration - Data Anonymization using DB Sync
entry.content:
This article describes how to use and configure anonymization feature of SAP Commerce DB Sync tool. Support for data anonymization was introduced in release v1.2.7 - it allows to alter the data on the fly during migration process based on provided configuration.

 

Prerequisites

DB Sync extensions has to be included in Customer's repository and properly configured for data migration. For more informations please refer to:

  • Commerce DB Sync - User Guide for Data Migration
  • SAP Commerce Cloud - Data Migration in Action

 

Configuration

Configuration is quite simple and consists of just two steps. Firstly, data anonymization has to be enabled, and then config file has to be adjusted to specify what data and how it will be altered during migration process. 

 

Enable anonymization feature

By default this is disabled, to enable set the following property:

 

migration.anonymizer.enabled=true

 

Configure how to anonymize the data

Configuration file is provided in SAP Commerce DB Sync tool, it can be found in commercedbsync extension under resources/anonymizer/config.yml. Out of the box it includes sample configuration for following tables:

  • users,
  • addresses,
  • emailaddress,
  • emailmessage,
  • useraudit,
  • pointofservice.

This should be a good starting point. However, it is important that you review and adjust this file to cover all necessary fields to ensure that data will be completely anonymized!

In this example we will use below OOTB configuration for tabes users and addresses:

 

tables:
  - name: users
    columns:
      - name: p_name
        operation: REPLACE
        text: "{GUID}-{RANDOM(number,5)}"
      - name: p_description
        operation: REPLACE
        text: "new description"
      - name: p_uid
        operation: REPLACE
        text: "{GUID}-{RANDOM(number,5)}"
        excludeRow: ["anonymous", "admin"]
      - name: p_originaluid
        operation: REPLACE
        text: "{GUID}-{RANDOM(number,5)}@test.com"
      - name: p_email
        operation: REPLACE
        text: "{GUID}-{RANDOM(number,5)}@test.com"

  - name: addresses
    columns:
      - name: p_cellphone
        operation: REPLACE
        text: "{RANDOM(number,10)}"
      - name: p_phone1
        operation: REPLACE
        text: "{RANDOM(number,10)}"
      - name: p_phone2
        operation: REPLACE
        text: "{RANDOM(number,10)}"
      - name: p_email
        operation: REPLACE
        text: "{GUID}-{RANDOM(string,5)}.{RANDOM(number,2)}@test.com"
        exclude: ["anette.customer@hybris.com", "keenreviewer24@hybris.com"]
      - name: p_fax
        operation: REPLACE
        text: "{RANDOM(number,10)}"
      - name: p_firstname
        operation: REPLACE
        text: "name_{RANDOM(number,5)}"
      - name: p_lastname
        operation: REPLACE
        text: "lastname_{RANDOM(number,5)}"
      - name: p_middlename
        operation: REPLACE
        text: "middlename_{RANDOM(number,5)}"
      - name: p_middlename2
        operation: REPLACE
        text: "middlename2_{RANDOM(number,5)}"
      - name: p_postalcode
        operation: REPLACE
        text: "{RANDOM(number,5)}"
      - name: p_streetname
        operation: REPLACE
        text: "{RANDOM(street)}"
      - name: p_streetnumber
        operation: REPLACE
        text: "{RANDOM(number,3)}"
      - name: p_town
        operation: REPLACE
        text: "{RANDOM(city)}"

 

By inspecting code above, you can see that it is possible to define how to alter data for specific column and also exclude certain values, or entire rows from the anonymization process.

In this example, in users table we don't want to alter any columns related to admin user. That way we still have a valid user that we can log in to after the data is migrated and anonymized.

Please also notice that for addresses random city and street is used. Those fields will be populated on randomly picked predefined data from commercedbsync/resources/anonymizer/address.json file.

For more information regarding anonymization configuration please refer to Anonymizer Documentation.

 

Running data migration with anonymization

Once anonymizer is enabled and configuration is in place there are no more extra steps - you can just start the migration from HAC and wait for the process to finish.

 

Validation

After successful migration it is necessary to validate that data has been properly anonymized. It can be done by running some queries or checking in the Backoffice.

 

Example before data anonymization (source db):

Table: users

PKp_uidp_namep_description
8796093054980adminAdministratoradministrator description
8796096364548canonlover@hybris.comCanon LoverTest description 1
8796096724996vipgiold@hybris.comCustomer VIP GoldTest description 2
8796096790532aaron.customer@hybris.comAaron CustomerTest description 3
8796096856068andrew.customer@hybris.comAndrew CustomerTest description 4

Table: addresses

PKp_phone1p_townp_streetname
8796093054999+81 4535 1332OsakaShinsaibashi
8796093087767+81 7684 8185TokioShinkawa Chuo
8796093120535+81 1114 9907YokohamaSakuragicho
8796093153303+81 4220 68YokohamaSumiyoshi-Cho
8796093186071+81 7093 958Tokio Higashi Shimbashi

 

Example after data anonymization (target db):

Table: users

PKp_uidp_namep_description
8796093054980adminAdministratoradministrator description
879609636454830d04a66-0cea-446c-875a-2b8dbc9e8564-437037c0f2cb1-76fc-4ee6-8bc8-629d7b75f9c9-31824new description
8796096724996749b7b2e-b987-49cc-839f-3d53ebd0dcfd-57335a3c2ca1e-c5bf-4678-aec8-bd69b3037277-18655new description
879609679053259fc93bf-3470-4855-9dac-0abaf3b883e0-69104a030c308-7fa3-4b77-a1f3-1ce6dc12fa41-70960new description
879609685606872e282f2-8ea4-437e-be0a-9b32fb6dec0f-188722a737580-6d47-4876-a136-86e83b5dded9-65694new description

Table: addresses

PKp_phone1p_townp_streetname
87960930549994654719409Andorra la VellaViali di Circonvallazione
87960930877676487859143PalermoClerkenwell Road
87960931205355821083594AmsterdamFriedrichstrabe
87960931533035577704800LeipzigPonte Vecchio
87960931860711970063394WarsawCorso Buenos Aires

entry.title: How SAP Support Fixed Infinite Loop Issues in Partner-Product Promotions
entry.content:
At SAP Support, we recently found a problem in some "Buy X Get X" promotions in SAP Commerce versions 2211.16 and 2211.17. When setting up partner-product promotions with identical product containers, the system sometimes ended up in an infinite loop. This caused high CPU usage and memory problems. The issue was traced back to a bug in Drools' logic.
In this blog, we’ll explain what went wrong, why it happened, and how to fix it.

What Was the Problem? Slow Performance and Infinite Loops

The issue mainly affected promotions like "Buy X Get X Discount" or "Buy X Get X Free." If two containers in the promotion had the same products or categories, Drools (the rules engine) couldn’t process them correctly.
This led to infinite loops in these parts of the system:

  • RuleNetworkEvaluator.doUpdatesReorderRightMemory
  • RuleNetworkEvaluator.doRiaNode2
  • RuleNetworkEvaluator.doUpdatesReorderLeftMemory

The infinite loop caused high CPU and memory usage. If your storefront slowed down—especially when changing product quantities—you may have experienced this issue. You can confirm it by checking the thread dump.

What Caused the Problem? A Bug in Drools

The problem happened because Drools couldn’t handle two containers with the same products or categories in the promotion rules. Instead of processing them properly, it got stuck in a loop, which used up system resources.

How to Fix It? Best Practices to Avoid Loops

We identified the problem and recommend the following steps to avoid it:

Don’t Use Identical Containers

Avoid creating promotions with two containers that have the same products or categories.
Instead, use one container in the Conditions area. Then, reference the same container for both "Qualifying Products" and "Partner Products" in the Actions area.

Upcoming Fix in Future Releases

SAP is working on a fix to stop users from creating these kinds of promotions by mistake.
This fix will be included in version 2211.22 of SAP Commerce Cloud.

Be Careful with Custom Actions

If you customize the promotion rules, make sure to fully consume all products (OrderEntries) that match the conditions.
Partially using the products can cause errors like NullPointerException
SAP reported this issue to the Drools team, and they’re working on it.

Final Thoughts

Following these steps can help you avoid infinite loop issues in your partner-product promotions. If you’re using versions 2211.16 or 2211.17, review your promotions to ensure they don’t include identical containers.
The fix coming in version 2211.22 will add extra protection against these errors. Until then, using the best practices outlined here will keep your SAP Commerce setup running smoothly.

entry.title: SAP Service Cloud version 2 Learning Updates and Stay Certified Release
entry.content:
Hello everyone, 

I am glad to announce the update to our learning journey Using and Implementing SAP Service Cloud version 2 and the release of its corresponding Stay Certified Course and Assessment. In this blog post, I shall endeavour to provide you with as much information as possible to help you start 2025 with the latest updates from Product Learning for SAP Service Cloud version 2. 

 

Using and Implementing SAP Service Cloud version 2

Overview

The course in this learning journey enables you to explain and demonstrate how to use and implement SAP Service Cloud Version 2 which is built using Cloud Native Stack. The course walks you through how the Administrator role can leverage the SAP Service Cloud Version 2 capabilities to both improve and scale up the service experience that a company can provide its customers. This perspective of the administrator role is also important for all other roles new to the solution, such as the business user, the solution consultant and the developer.

Learning objectives

After completing this course, you will be able to:

  • Make the case for SAP Service Cloud Version 2
  • Follow a day in the life of the service employee
  • Start the implementation
  • Perform master data setup
  • Create service objects
  • Set up case management
  • Configure service elements
  • Configure communication channels
  • Overview how SAP Service Cloud Version 2 can be integrated into the broader, SAP Cloud for Customer ecosystem

Upon successful completion of the course, you may wish to take the corresponding Certification Exam 

SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP Service Cloud Version 2

 

Stay Certified- SAP Service Cloud version 2

Stay certified is a program that ensures that you are kept up to date with your certification as well as updates to product features and functions. A Stay Certified course comprises of the following: 

  • An online course which contains all the information about the updates, changes and new features to the SAP Service Cloud version 2 solution.  
  • An assessment which checks your knowledge on the latest features. When you feel comfortable with the material, you can take the assessment. If you pass it successfully, your certification will be valid for one year.

We hope that with the updates to the learning content, you can get your 2025 off to a great start! 

Regards,

Jameel
